dev_plans.content: Lightweight heuristic approach using NumPy and scikit-learn directly. Implement a `FairnessProbe` class that hooks into the validation loop after each epoch. Steps: (1) During validation, collect model predictions (probabilities), true labels, and ethnicity group labels into three parallel arrays. (2) After the validation pass, iterate over the 6 ethnicity groups (White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Native American, Other). For each group, slice the arrays and compute AUROC using `sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score`, AUPRC via `average_precision_score`, sensitivity/specificity at threshold 0.5, and Brier score. (3) Compute the max-min AUROC gap across groups as the primary disparity signal. (4) Log per-group metrics to a CSV file (`fairness_per_epoch.csv`) with columns: epoch, group, auroc, auprc, sensitivity, specificity, brier, n_samples. (5) Append the epoch's max-min gap to a scalar log for plotting. (6) At end of training, produce a matplotlib line plot of max-min AUROC gap per epoch with a horizontal threshold line at 0.05. Handle empty subgroups by skipping groups with fewer than 10 positive examples and logging a warning. Output artifacts: CSV log, line plot PNG, and optional TensorBoard scalars. This approach is fast (~seconds per epoch), has no extra dependencies beyond scikit-learn, and is easy to integrate but does not quantify uncertainty.
dev_plans.metric: Max-min AUROC gap across the 6 ethnicity subgroups per epoch (float in [0, 1])
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — Chen et al. and the Fairlearn community treat a 5-percentage-point AUROC gap as the conventional boundary between acceptable subgroup variation and clinically meaningful disparity. Gaps below this are typically within bootstrap noise for realistic cohort sizes; gaps above indicate systematic bias warranting intervention.

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous approach using stratified bootstrapping to quantify uncertainty given small minority subgroup sizes. Implement `BootstrapFairnessProbe` invoked once per epoch on the validation set. Steps: (1) Collect predictions, labels, and ethnicity group IDs after each validation pass. (2) For each of the 6 ethnicity groups, perform B=1000 stratified bootstrap resamples: sample with replacement within each group preserving the class balance (separate resampling of positives and negatives to avoid degenerate folds). (3) For each resample, compute AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity at fixed specificity=0.9, specificity at fixed sensitivity=0.9, and Brier score. Accumulate into per-group distributions. (4) Compute 95% percentile confidence intervals per group per metric. (5) Compute disparity statistics: max-min AUROC gap, standard deviation of group AUROCs, equalized odds difference (max absolute difference in TPR and FPR at threshold 0.5). (6) Run a permutation test on group labels (N=500 permutations) to produce a p-value for the null hypothesis that AUROC is equal across groups. (7) Log all statistics to a structured JSONL file per epoch and emit a forest plot showing per-group AUROC with CI whiskers. (8) The scalar time-series metric is the max-min AUROC gap, with the lower bound of its bootstrap CI also logged. (9) Draw the 0.05 threshold line on the gap plot; flag epochs where the CI lower bound exceeds the threshold as statistically significant disparity. Use `scipy.stats` for permutation testing and `joblib.Parallel` to parallelize bootstraps across cores. Artifacts: JSONL log, forest plot per epoch, gap-vs-epoch plot with CI ribbon and threshold line, alert file if threshold is breached with significance.
dev_plans.metric: Max-min AUROC gap across ethnicity groups per epoch, with bootstrap 95% CI (the point estimate is the plotted float)
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — Seyyed-Kalantari et al. (Nature Medicine 2021) and standard clinical ML fairness audits adopt 0.05 AUROC as the disparity threshold. Because we also compute bootstrap CIs, the threshold is meaningful only when combined with significance: a point estimate above 0.05 whose CI lower bound also exceeds 0.05 indicates robust disparity.

dev_plans.content: Tooling/library-based approach leveraging Microsoft's Fairlearn toolkit and MLflow for experiment tracking. Implement `FairlearnProbe` that wraps Fairlearn's `MetricFrame` and integrates with the training loop via an epoch-end callback (PyTorch Lightning `Callback` or HuggingFace `TrainerCallback`). Steps: (1) Install dependencies: `fairlearn>=0.10`, `mlflow`, `pandas`. (2) On each validation-end event, gather predictions, labels, and ethnicity groups into a pandas DataFrame. (3) Define a metrics dict for `MetricFrame`: `{'auroc': roc_auc_score, 'auprc': average_precision_score, 'sensitivity': recall_score, 'specificity': specificity_fn, 'brier': brier_score_loss}`. (4) Instantiate `MetricFrame(metrics=metrics_dict, y_true=labels, y_pred=probs, sensitive_features=ethnicity)`. Call `.by_group` to get per-group metrics and `.difference(method='between_groups')` for disparity. (5) Use Fairlearn's `equalized_odds_difference` and `demographic_parity_difference` helpers for standardized disparity measures. (6) Log all per-group metrics and disparities to MLflow as both parameters (per-epoch) and artifacts (full DataFrame per epoch as CSV). (7) Use MLflow's built-in plotting to render time-series of the max-min AUROC gap across epochs, with a client-side overlay of the 0.05 reference line via a custom `mlflow.log_figure` call that uses matplotlib to draw `axhline(0.05, color='red', linestyle='--')`. (8) Register an alert rule: if max-min gap > 0.05 for 3 consecutive epochs, write a `fairness_alert.json` artifact and optionally trigger a Slack webhook. (9) Expose the probe results via MLflow UI for stakeholder review; compare runs across hyperparameter sweeps to identify training configurations that minimize disparity. Artifacts: MLflow run with per-epoch metrics, per-group CSV artifacts, time-series plot with threshold, alert file. This approach is heaviest on dependencies but provides a production-grade auditable trail and aligns with regulatory expectations for healthcare AI.
dev_plans.metric: Fairlearn `MetricFrame.difference(method='between_groups')` applied to AUROC — equivalent to max-min AUROC gap across ethnicity subgroups, logged to MLflow per epoch
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — Fairlearn documentation and the MIMIC-III fairness benchmarks (Harutyunyan et al. 2019) use 0.05 AUROC difference as the default operational threshold for subgroup parity. It matches FDA-aligned healthcare AI auditing guidance and is the standard cited in fairness regulatory literature.
